topic: Flagship topic with hand-derived nonroutine object: sharp influence-function non-equivalence frontier for four named staggered-DiD estimators: Borusyak-Jaravel-Spiess imputation (ReStud 2024), Sun-Abraham interaction-weighted (JoE 2021), Callaway-Sant'Anna group-time doubly robust (JoE 2021), and Wooldridge cohort-time saturated TWFE (2021).

open_problems.open_problem: Characterize the exact first-order influence-function equivalence frontier for BJS imputation, Sun-Abraham interaction-weighted, Callaway-Sant'Anna group-time doubly robust, and Wooldridge cohort-time saturated TWFE when all four are aggregated to the same event-time ATT target.

source_refs.claim: BJS motivate imputation by noting robust staggered-DiD estimators existed but their efficiency properties were not known and alternatives use less pre-treatment information.
source_refs.author: Borusyak, Jaravel, and Spiess

source_refs.claim: Callaway-Sant'Anna separate identification of group-time ATTs, aggregation, and estimation/inference for multi-period staggered DiD.
source_refs.author: Callaway and Sant'Anna

source_refs.claim: Sun-Abraham show dynamic TWFE coefficients can be contaminated by treatment effects from other relative periods and propose interaction-weighted event-study estimation.
source_refs.author: Sun and Abraham

source_refs.claim: Wooldridge develops extended TWFE/POLS estimators for cohort-time ATTs and notes imputation equivalence in particular complete-panel designs.
source_refs.author: Wooldridge

source_refs.excerpt: BJS and estimator m are influence-function equivalent for theta_a if and only if Gamma_m(pi,Sigma,a)tau=0
Conjecture... is not yet proved; the main open issue is necessity of the specific Gamma_m construction

open_problems.why_unsolved: The published literature gives identification and estimation results estimator-by-estimator, and BJS establish efficiency for imputation, but neither the papers nor the failed prior proposal close a necessary-and-sufficient primitive condition under which all four named procedures have the same first-order influence-function projection after being forced onto one ATT aggregation; the prior section 11 specifically leaves necessity of the Gamma_m construction unproved.
open_problems.why_nontrivial: The natural attempt to compare only cohort-event aggregation weights fails because BJS and Wooldridge state point-estimate equivalences only for restricted estimand/design classes while the prior section 11 says membership in ker Gamma_m also depends on the identified ATT vector tau.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A finite-dimensional theorem constructing an observable contrast matrix C(pi,Sigma,G,T) such that the four estimators have first-order equivalent influence functions if and only if C tau = 0, with the corresponding variance formula off the kernel.

open_problems.open_problem: Give a sharp closed-form variance-gap formula and a minimal finite staggered-design witness in which BJS, Sun-Abraham, Callaway-Sant'Anna, and Wooldridge identify the same ATT but have different influence-function projections.

source_refs.claim: BJS report confidence-interval gains for imputation relative to other robust estimators and attribute efficiency gains to using all valid pre-treatment information.
source_refs.author: Borusyak, Jaravel, and Spiess

source_refs.claim: de Chaisemartin-D'Haultfoeuille show TWFE can place negative weights on heterogeneous group-period treatment effects, separating regression variation from target effects.
source_refs.author: de Chaisemartin and D'Haultfoeuille

source_refs.claim: Goodman-Bacon decomposes timing-varying DiD into 2x2 comparisons and shows bias arises when treatment effects vary over time.
source_refs.author: Goodman-Bacon

source_refs.excerpt: Add a concrete three-period, two-cohort worked matrix for Gamma_m and show which known estimator pair separates
Add a small T=3 or T=4 symbolic example showing K_e positive for one named estimator and zero for another

open_problems.why_unsolved: Existing papers establish that robust alternatives can differ, that TWFE weights can be pathological, and that BJS is efficient in its class, while the prior reviewers explicitly asked for a finite symbolic witness; no source supplies a primitive normed variance-gap identity across the four named modern estimators or a minimal 3-cohort by 4-period example isolating equal estimands from unequal influence functions.
open_problems.why_nontrivial: The natural attempt to invoke BJS efficiency directly fails because efficiency ranks BJS against admissible unbiased estimators but reviewers requested a T=3 or T=4 worked matrix to show the diagnostic is not a relabelled generic projection residual.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A strict-gap identity of the form Var(IF_alt)-Var(IF_BJS)=||C tau||^2_Omega plus an explicit 3-cohort by 4-period numerical witness with cohort shares, covariance blocks, estimator loadings, C tau values, and unequal IF variance.

open_problems.open_problem: Define the unique computable projection-loss object and corrected projection map under which Wooldridge cohort-time saturated TWFE recovers the BJS efficient imputation influence function, and characterize the primitive failures outside that class.

source_refs.claim: Wooldridge gives pooled-regression and ETWFE/imputation equivalences under canonical-link or linear complete-panel settings.
source_refs.author: Wooldridge

source_refs.claim: BJS state Wooldridge-style imputation equivalence holds only for a restricted class of estimands in complete panels with time-invariant controls.
source_refs.author: Borusyak, Jaravel, and Spiess

source_refs.excerpt: K_star=0 corner case undefined, Z_M projection loss not uniquely specified across the 4 estimators
Add an explicit block defining the SA, CS, Wooldridge, and BJS vectorized nuisance-design matrices `Z_M`

open_problems.why_unsolved: Wooldridge gives regression/imputation equivalences and BJS delimit those equivalences, while the bank README identifies non-unique Z_M projection loss as the prior proposal blocker; the missing object is the exact finite-dimensional projection operator mapping cohort-time saturated regression residuals into the BJS influence function and the matrix restrictions under which that operator ceases to be efficient.
open_problems.why_nontrivial: The natural attempt to cite Wooldridge's ETWFE-imputation equivalence fails because BJS limits that equivalence to restricted complete-panel/time-invariant-control settings and the bank README says Z_M projection loss was not uniquely specified across the four estimators.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A definition-and-projection theorem constructing BJS, SA, CS, and Wooldridge nuisance-design/incidence matrices with fixed dimensions and proving a unique residual projection contrast computable from cohort shares, support, and covariance primitives.

open_problems.open_problem: Determine when modern event-study diagnostics from BJS imputation, Callaway-Sant'Anna aggregation, Sun-Abraham interaction weights, and dynamic TWFE/ETWFE have first-order equivalent influence functions for pre-period and post-period coefficients.

source_refs.claim: Roth shows default event-study plots from recent DiD methods can differ even without staggered timing because pre- and post-treatment coefficients are constructed asymmetrically.
source_refs.author: Roth

source_refs.claim: Roth-Sant'Anna-Bilinski-Poe emphasize that insignificant pre-trend tests need not validate parallel trends because power can be low.
source_refs.author: Roth, Sant'Anna, Bilinski, and Poe

source_refs.claim: Sun-Abraham identify contamination of event-study lead/lag coefficients under heterogeneous dynamic treatment effects.
source_refs.author: Sun and Abraham

source_refs.excerpt: when additional pre-treatment periods change the efficient influence function rather than serving only as diagnostics
the finite-dimensional rank certificate that decides whether the extra pre-period restrictions... are active

open_problems.why_unsolved: Recent work documents contamination, low power, and asymmetric event-study construction, while the rejected angle frames extra pre-period restrictions as an active-rank phenomenon that can alter the efficient influence function; no source gives a unified IF-level condition for when diagnostic coefficients produced by the four named estimators are equivalent rather than only visually comparable.
open_problems.why_nontrivial: The natural attempt to compare plotted event-time coefficients fails because Roth shows different methods can produce different plot shapes on the same data and the rejected angle says extra pre-periods may change the efficient influence function through active restrictions.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A coefficient-level IF equivalence criterion for pre-period and post-period event-study diagnostics, separating target-effect differences from estimator-projection differences through a finite rank or projection condition.

open_problems.open_problem: Rebase the BJS/non-BJS frontier against the published efficient influence-function benchmark rather than assuming BJS as the baseline, and characterize when BJS, SA, CS, and Wooldridge jointly attain that bound.

source_refs.excerpt: Rebase the frontier on ChenSantAnnaXie2025's EIF: define Gamma_m as the gap between each named estimator IF
ChenSantAnnaXie2025 is underplayed: it derives closed-form EIFs and efficient variance bounds

open_problems.why_unsolved: The reviewer warns that existing closed-form EIF work may already supply the efficient bound geometry; the unresolved problem is to separate a new quotient-equality frontier from a corollary of published EIF weights and to state when each named estimator, and all four jointly, attain the efficient bound.
open_problems.why_nontrivial: The natural attempt to take BJS as the efficient baseline fails because the reviewer says ChenSantAnnaXie2025 derives closed-form EIFs and efficient variance bounds that the proposal underplays.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A projection theorem mapping each named estimator's influence function to the published EIF benchmark and giving necessary-and-sufficient conditions for each estimator, and all four jointly, to attain the efficient bound.

literature_map:
Borusyak-Jaravel-Spiess (ReStud 2024) derive a robust finite-sample efficient imputation estimator and frame earlier robust staggered-DiD estimators as having unknown efficiency properties.
Sun-Abraham (JoE 2021) show dynamic TWFE lead/lag coefficients can be contaminated by treatment effects from other relative periods and propose interaction-weighted event-study estimation.
Callaway-Sant'Anna (JoE 2021) build a group-time ATT framework separating identification, aggregation, and estimation/inference, including doubly robust estimands.
Wooldridge (2021 working paper; Econometrics Journal 2023) develops saturated/extended TWFE and pooled-regression approaches that can coincide with imputation in restricted settings.
de Chaisemartin-D'Haultfoeuille (AER 2020) and Goodman-Bacon (JoE 2021) provide negative-weight and timing-comparison diagnostics explaining why standard TWFE can fail under heterogeneous effects.
Roth-Sant'Anna-Bilinski-Poe (JoE 2023) synthesize modern DiD and stress that pre-trend tests and estimator choice involve distinct identifying and inferential issues.
Gardner's two-stage DiD reinforces the imputation/projection view: untreated observations estimate fixed effects, then treated residuals identify average effects.
BJS note some imputation-like estimators coincide for specific estimand classes, while their efficiency and robustness claims are not limited to those classes.
The closest live gap is not identification of group-time ATTs, which is well covered, but IF-level equivalence and variance comparison after valid estimators are forced onto the same ATT target.
Roth's event-study note adds a diagnostic angle: modern methods can generate different pre/post plot shapes on the same data because coefficients are constructed asymmetrically.
Across these papers, the unresolved object is a finite primitive contrast matrix linking cohort shares, untreated covariance structure, event-time aggregation, and IF projections.
A useful proposer substrate is therefore a panel-methods open problem about exact IF frontiers and strict variance gaps, not another generic FWL or negative-weight decomposition.
prior_proposal_map:
Mined the closest failed banked proposal CausalSmith/doc/formalization/_bank/failed/eid_bjs_efficient_event_study_v1.
The README says the kernel nearly cleared flagship but failed on undefined K_star=0 and non-unique Z_M projection loss.
The final proposal's section 8 conjecture states a Gamma_m(pi,Sigma,a)tau=0 IF-equivalence frontier and a quotient variance-gap formula.
The final proposal's section 11 honest scope flags say necessity of the specific Gamma_m construction remains unproved and the spectral-threshold-only K_star framing was withdrawn.
Rejected angle0 contains the abandoned covariance-whitened row-space/spectral active-rank formulation.
Rejected angle1 contains the estimator-specific active-score-rank formulation and the multiple-preperiod active-restriction gap.
Reviewer JSONs yielded high-signal strengthening paths: define Z_M/B_m matrices, fix Gamma scaling, add worked T=3/T=4 witnesses, and rebase against ChenSantAnnaXie2025 EIFs.
Study-pipeline LATE, Manski-bounds, and interval-regression gaps were reviewed as augmenting inputs but not emitted because they are outside this panel DiD topic anchor.
